name: Spermidine elderly CAD phase 2 trial is active with no posted results
summary: NCT06186102 is a large older-patient spermidine trial with autophagy, polyamine, muscle, cardiometabolic, and cognition endpoints, but it has no posted results.
statement: ClinicalTrials.gov lists NCT06186102 as an active-not-recruiting 180-participant phase 2 spermidine trial in elderly coronary-artery-disease patients with physical-performance, muscle, inflammation, cognition, autophagy-marker, polyamine, vascular, and cardiovascular endpoints and no posted results in the June 11, 2026 API view.
population_or_model: Elderly patients with coronary artery disease and related cardiometabolic conditions
time_horizon: Twelve-month intervention with estimated study completion in January 2027
quantitative_note: Estimated enrollment is 180; registry status is active not recruiting; result status is not posted.
caveats: No-results registry status is not evidence of benefit.
The disease-context trial may not generalize to healthy aging.
Autophagy markers are part of a broad cardiometabolic and functional endpoint set, so causal attribution may remain difficult even after results post.
